title: Pre-close gate + effort calibration loop
current_state: Closing a ticket today means moving its JSON from tickets/open/ to tickets/closed/ with status flipped to closed. No enforcement of: verify.py PASS, SOLUTIONS.jsonl entry recorded, ADR written when adr_required is set, no new orphan TODOs introduced. Tickets carry effort_estimate but never effort_actual — no learning loop for calibration.
structural_problem: A close-ticket workflow that's manual file-moves drifts from intent. Each missing gate is a single point of failure: ADRs get forgotten, verify gets skipped under pressure, solutions don't get logged, estimates never get learned.
target_state: scripts/close_ticket.py <T-NNN> is the sanctioned close path. Runs four gates in sequence: (1) verify.py PASS, (2) SOLUTIONS.jsonl has entry for T-NNN OR --no-solution flag set, (3) if adr_required is set, ADR file exists, (4) no new bare-except / TODO without ticket in modified files since ticket created. Prompts for effort_actual_minutes and writes to ticket JSON. Moves file. Refuses on any gate failure.
files_affected: tickets/SCHEMA.md (NEW or UPDATE — document the gate)
Optional: pre-commit hook that warns on manual ticket moves
audit_refs: Phase 8.7 had 12 tickets closed but only some have linked solutions; ADR-006 was almost forgotten
design_notes: Gates list is a dataclass — easy to add (e.g., 'tests added for ticket scope')
Solution check: grep SOLUTIONS.jsonl for ticket_id == T-NNN
ADR check: read ticket's adr_required field, glob docs/adr/<num>-*.md
Effort field: add effort_actual_minutes to ticket JSON (alongside effort_estimate)
Weekly retro (retro.py) can later compute estimate-vs-actual bias per ticket type
--force flag bypasses gates with stderr warning + track_silent record
Idempotent: if ticket already in closed/, exits 0 with 'already closed'
success_criteria: Running close_ticket.py with missing solution → exit 1 with 'no solution recorded for T-NNN'
Running close_ticket.py with all gates green → moves file, writes effort_actual_minutes
After 10 ticket closes, retro.py can compute estimate vs actual deltas
risk_notes: Friction risk: gate too strict, contributors bypass with --force. Mitigation: --force always recorded; weekly report on bypass count. Migration risk: existing closed tickets lack effort_actual; accept as historical baseline.
migration_plan: 1. Write scripts/close_ticket.py with four gate functions
3. Tests: temp ticket fixtures, each gate failure mode
4. Update CLAUDE.md / PI.md to reference the new close path
5. (Future T-129?) wire retro.py to compute estimate-vs-actual bias
